name: Clarence Profit
abstract: Clarence Profit (June 26, 1912 – October 22, 1944) was a jazz pianist and composer associated with swing. Profit was born in New York, United States.

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: Clarence Profit wrote them things like [SINGS "STOMPIN AT THE SAVOY"]... Thelonious was patterning...kind of patterning his playing after Clarence Profit.

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: I heard Clarence Profit play. He played stride piano. Oh, God, that's the first time I heard piano played like that.

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: I come in and slap him in the face in his own group on his gig
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
